problem.passage:
6. Read the passage in <A> and the sentences in <B>, and
follow the directions.【5 points】
<A>
Anaphors such as each other have to be bound by their
be antecedent. An anaphor must satisfy two conditions to
bound. An anaphor can be bound if it is coindexed (i.e.,
coreferential) with its antecedent and is also c-commanded by
that antecedent within the smallest clause or noun phrase
containing the anaphor. A node c-commands its sister and all
the descendents of its sister. For example, in (1), B
c-commands C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J, and K; however, I
c-commands only H, which is its sister. It does not
c-command any other nodes.
(1)
Consider the two structures for the verb phrase in the
double object construction (2). (3) is a ternary (three)
branching structure, which is a kind of multiple branching
structure, and (4) a binary branching structure.
(2) Tom will give Mary a book.
(3) (4)
In (3) and (4), the direct and indirect objects have different
structural relations.
<B>
each other. (i) Mary showed the boys
i i
(ii) *Mary showed each other the boys.
i i
(In the examples the boys and each other refer to the
same people.)
Note: *indicates that the sentence is ungrammatical.
Identify which VP structure, (3) or (4), can account for the
ungrammaticality of sentence (ii) in <B>. Then, explain why one,
but not the other structure, can account for the ungrammaticality
by using the c-command relation described in <A>.
영 어 [전공B]
